babyDevelopment: The baby's brain is continuing to develop rapidly. The bones in the head are staying soft to allow for a safer birth through the birth canal. The immune system is maturing as antibodies from the mother are passed to the baby.
bodyChanges: You might feel more pressure in your pelvic area as the baby moves lower. Some women experience 'insomnia' due to physical discomfort and anxiety. Heartburn may be more persistent as the uterus pushes into the stomach.
tips: Practice relaxation and meditation to improve sleep quality
Consider a prenatal class if you haven't attended one
Discuss your labor and delivery preferences with your provider

dont: Stay in one position for too long

travelAdvisory.note: Avoid long-distance travel from now until delivery.

warningSignsToWatch: Redness or localized heat on breasts
milestoneDescription: Your baby is receiving valuable antibodies from you to boost its immune system.
